svg.text: HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Wiring — Raspberry Pi 40-pin Header
Numbers in circles = physical header pins · GPIO = BCM numbering (as in config.py)
Board viewed from above: pin 1 (3V3) is the corner pin nearest the microSD-card end
GPIO 12 — motor L PWM, in use
GPIO 13 — motor R PWM, in use
GPIO 16 — motor L DIR, in use
GPIO 20 — motor R DIR, in use
(colors follow the standard 10-wire Dupont ribbon order)
• All 5 sensors run on 3.3V — VCC to 3V3 (pins 1/17).
• ECHO connects directly to GPIO; no level shifter needed.
• GND of all sensors → any Ground pin (6, 9, 14, 20, 25, 30, 34, 39).
• Grayed pins are already occupied: motors (GPIO 12, 13, 16, 20) and IMU I2C (GPIO 2, 3) — do not reuse them.
• GPIO 1 (back_left TRIG) is also ID_SC, the HAT EEPROM ID pin: fine as GPIO here, but free it if a HAT is ever stacked.
